'''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえき・ぶぜんおこしかけ）は[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]の[[国道10号]]と[[椎田道路|椎田道路(無料区間)]]の分岐上にある道の駅。
==概要== *1992年 豊前青年会議所が[[道の駅大栄]]を視察 *2000年3月 開駅 *照明学会照明普及会より2000年の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞 *名称の一部にもなっている「おこしかけ」は神功皇后がこの地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因んでいる
==道路== *[[国道10号]] *[[椎田道路|椎田道路(無料区間)]]
==施設== *駐車場：（大型車10台、普通車76台、身障者専用6台） *公衆電話 *トイレ：（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） *公園 *休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） *自動販売機 *情報コーナー：（7:00～19:00） *直売所：（8:00～21:00） *屋台村：（10:00～18:00）
==その他== 2000年3月4日開駅
==関連項目== *[[道の駅一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/index.html 九州「道の駅」ガイド（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] *[http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/kitakyu/t_index.html 北九州国道事務所] *[http://www.city.buzen.fukuoka.jp/ 豊前市] *[http://www.buzen-cci.or.jp/index.htm 豊前商工会議所]
[[category:道の駅 九州地方|ぶぜんおこしかけ]]

8:00～18:00（11～3月）| 郵便番号=828-0011| 都道府県名=福岡県| 郡名=| 自治体名=豊前市| 所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1| 電話番号=0979-84-0544| 自治体公式サイト=http://www.city.buzen.fukuoka.jp/| 国土交通省=http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html| }} '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえきぶぜんおこしかけ）は[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸の[[国道10号]]と[[椎田道路|椎田道路(無料区間)]]の分岐上にある[[道の駅]]。国道10号で初の道の駅として2000年に開駅した。 ==道路== *[[国道10号]] *[[椎田道路|椎田道路（無料区間）]] ==施設== * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 *[[公衆電話]] *公園 *休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） *自動販売機 *情報コーナー：（7:00～19:00） *直売所：（8:00～21:00） *屋台村：（10:00～18:00） ==休館日== *1月に2日ほど休業有 ==歴史== *[[1992年]] - 豊前青年会議所が[[道の駅大栄]]を視察。 *[[2000年]]3月 - 開駅。 ==その他== *照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞 *「おこしかけ」は[[神功皇后]]が巡幸の途中、当地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因んでいる ==関連項目== *[[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] *[[道の駅一覧 は行]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/index.html 九州「道の駅」ガイド（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] *[http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/kitakyu/t_index.html 北九州国道事務所] *[http://www.buzen-cci.or.jp/index.htm 豊前商工会議所] [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市|ふせんおこしかけ]]

{{道の駅| 登録番号=40002| 名称=豊前おこしかけ| 画像=[[画像:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px]]| 道路名=[[国道10号]]| 登録回=第14回| 登録年=[[1998年]]| 登録月日=[[4月17日]]| 開駅年=[[2000年]]| 開駅月日=[[3月]]| 事務所開設時間=8:00～19:00（4～10月）
8:00～18:00（11～3月）| 郵便番号=828-0011| 都道府県名=福岡県| 郡名=| 自治体名=豊前市| 所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1| 電話番号=0979-84-0544| 自治体公式サイト=http://www.city.buzen.fukuoka.jp/| 国土交通省=http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html| }} '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえきぶぜんおこしかけ）は[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸の[[国道10号]]と[[椎田道路|椎田道路(無料区間)]]の分岐上にある[[道の駅]]。国道10号で初の道の駅として2000年に開駅した。 ==道路== *[[国道10号]] *[[椎田道路|椎田道路（無料区間）]] ==施設== [[画像:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|250px|right|thumb|人手で賑わう道の駅豊前おこしかけ（2007年10月）]] * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 *[[公衆電話]] *公園 *休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） *自動販売機 *情報コーナー：（7:00～19:00） *直売所：（8:00～21:00） *屋台村：（10:00～18:00） ==休館日== *1月に2日ほど休業有 ==歴史== *[[1992年]] - 豊前青年会議所が[[道の駅大栄]]を視察。 *[[2000年]]3月 - 開駅。 ==その他== *照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞 *「おこしかけ」は[[神功皇后]]が巡幸の途中、当地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因んでいる ==関連項目== *[[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] *[[道の駅一覧 は行]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/index.html 九州「道の駅」ガイド（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] *[http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/kitakyu/t_index.html 北九州国道事務所] *[http://www.buzen-cci.or.jp/index.htm 豊前商工会議所] [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市|ふせんおこしかけ]]

revision.text:
{{道の駅 |登録番号=40002 |名称=豊前おこしかけ |画像=[[ファイル:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px]] |道路名=[[国道10号]] |登録回=第14回 |登録年=[[1998年]] |登録月日=[[4月17日]] |開駅年=[[2000年]] |開駅月日=[[3月]] |事務所開設時間=8:00 - 19:00（4 - 10月）
8:00 - 18:00（11 - 3月） |郵便番号=828-0011 |都道府県名=福岡県 |郡名= |自治体名=豊前市 |所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1
{{ウィキ座標度分秒|33|36|50.78|N|131|6|3.37|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |自治体公式サイト=http://www.city.buzen.fukuoka.jp/ |国土交通省=http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html }} [[ファイル:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|250px|right|thumb|人出で賑わう道の駅豊前おこしかけ（2007年10月）]] '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえき ぶぜんおこしかけ）は[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸の[[国道10号]]と[[椎田道路|椎田道路（無料区間）]]の分岐上にある[[道の駅]]。国道10号で初の道の駅として[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）3月に開駅した。 名称の「おこしかけ」は[[神功皇后]]が巡幸の途中、当地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因む。照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞した。 == 道路 == * [[国道10号]] * [[椎田道路|椎田道路（無料区間）]] == 施設 == * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 * [[公衆電話]] * 公園 * 休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） * 自動販売機 * 情報コーナー：（7:00 - 19:00） * 直売所：（8:00 - 21:00） * 屋台村：（10:00 - 18:00） == 休館日 == * 1月に2日ほど休業あり == 関連項目 == * [[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] * [[道の駅一覧 は行]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/index.html 九州「道の駅」ガイド（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] * [http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/kitakyu/t_index.html 北九州国道事務所] * [http://www.buzen-cci.or.jp/index.htm 豊前商工会議所] {{国道10号}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みちのえきふせんおこしかけ}} [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市]]

{{道の駅 |登録番号=40002 |名称=豊前おこしかけ |愛称= |画像=[[ファイル:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] |道路名=[[国道10号]] |登録回=第14回 |登録年=[[1998年]] |登録月日=[[4月17日]] |開駅年=[[2000年]] |開駅月日=[[3月]] |事務所開設時間=8:00 - 19:00（4 - 10月）
8:00 - 18:00（11 - 3月） |郵便番号=828-0011 |都道府県名=福岡県 |郡名= |自治体名=豊前市 |所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1 |座標={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|36|50.8|N|131|6|3.4|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |国土交通省=http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html |公式サイト=有 }} [[ファイル:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|人出で賑わう道の駅豊前おこしかけ（2007年10月）]] '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえき ぶぜんおこしかけ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸にある[[国道10号]]の[[道の駅]]である。 国道10号で初の道の駅として[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）3月に開駅した。 名称の「おこしかけ」は[[神功皇后]]が巡幸の途中、当地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因む。照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞した。 == 施設 == * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 * [[公衆電話]] * 公園 * 休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） * 自動販売機 * 情報コーナー：（7:00 - 19:00） * 直売所：（8:00 - 21:00） * 屋台村：（10:00 - 18:00） == 休館日 == * 1月に2日ほど休業あり == アクセス == * [[国道10号]] - 登録路線 ** [[東九州自動車道]] （[[椎田道路]]）- [[椎田南インターチェンジ|椎田南IC]]椎田南ICは、北九州方面のみの[[ハーフインターチェンジ|ハーフIC]]のため、大分方面からは[[豊前インターチェンジ|豊前IC]]経由。 ** [[福岡県道113号中津豊前線]] == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] * [[道の駅一覧 は行]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|name=豊前おこしかけ}} * [http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/ 九州「道の駅」ガイド（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] {{国道10号}} {{デフォルトソート:みちのえきふせんおこしかけ}} [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ|せんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市]] [[Category:2000年開業の施設]]

{{道の駅 |登録番号=40002 |名称=豊前おこしかけ |愛称= |画像=[[ファイル:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] |道路名=[[国道10号]] |登録回=第14回 |登録年=[[1998年]] |登録月日=[[4月17日]] |開駅年=[[2000年]] |開駅月日=[[3月]] |事務所開設時間=8:00 - 19:00（4 - 10月）
8:00 - 18:00（11 - 3月） |郵便番号=828-0011 |都道府県名=福岡県 |郡名= |自治体名=豊前市 |所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1 |座標={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|36|50.8|N|131|6|3.4|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |国土交通省=http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html |公式サイト=有 }} [[ファイル:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|人出で賑わう道の駅豊前おこしかけ（2007年10月）]] '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえき ぶぜんおこしかけ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸にある[[国道10号]]の[[道の駅]]である。 国道10号で初の道の駅として[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）3月に開駅した。 名称の「おこしかけ」は当地を通りかかる自動車が、特殊な進入経路から事故を起こしかけることから名付けられた。照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞した。 == 施設 == * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 * [[公衆電話]] * 公園 * 休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） * 自動販売機 * 情報コーナー：（7:00 - 19:00） * 直売所：（8:00 - 21:00） * 屋台村：（10:00 - 18:00） == 休館日 == * 1月に2日ほど休業あり == アクセス == * [[国道10号]] - 登録路線 ** [[東九州自動車道]] （[[椎田道路]]）- [[椎田南インターチェンジ|椎田南IC]]椎田南ICは、北九州方面のみの[[ハーフインターチェンジ|ハーフIC]]のため、大分方面からは[[豊前インターチェンジ|豊前IC]]経由。 ** [[福岡県道113号中津豊前線]] == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] * [[道の駅一覧 は行]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|name=豊前おこしかけ}} * [http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/ 九州「道の駅」ガイド（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] {{国道10号}} {{デフォルトソート:みちのえきふせんおこしかけ}} [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ|せんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市]] [[Category:2000年開業の施設]]

8:00 - 18:00（11 - 3月） |都道府県名=福岡県 |自治体名=豊前市 |所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1 |座標={{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |国土交通省= }} [[ファイル:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|人出で賑わう道の駅豊前おこしかけ（2007年10月）]] '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえき ぶぜんおこしかけ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸にある[[国道10号]]の[[道の駅]]である。 国道10号で初の道の駅として[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）3月に開駅した。 名称の「おこしかけ」は[[神功皇后]]が巡幸の途中、当地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因む。照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞した。 2016年4月、[[東九州自動車道]][[椎田南インターチェンジ]] - [[豊前インターチェンジ]]間が開通したことにより前年比で、利用客数は42.5%減、売上も39.9%減と激減した{{Cite news |title= 道の駅・豊前おこしかけ 来店客が激減 東九州道開通で素通り |newspaper=毎日新聞 |date=2016-06-16 |author=降旗英峰 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}}。 == 施設 == * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 * [[公衆電話]] * 公園 * 休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） * 自動販売機 * 情報コーナー：（7:00 - 19:00） * 直売所：（8:00 - 21:00） * 屋台村：（10:00 - 18:00） == 休館日 == * 1月に2日ほど休業あり == アクセス == * [[国道10号]] - 登録路線 ** {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[東九州自動車道]] （[[椎田道路]]）- [[椎田南インターチェンジ|椎田南IC]]椎田南ICは、北九州方面のみの[[ハーフインターチェンジ|ハーフIC]]のため、大分方面からは[[豊前インターチェンジ|豊前IC]]経由。 ** [[福岡県道113号中津豊前線]] == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] * [[道の駅一覧 は行]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|name=豊前おこしかけ}} * [http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/ 九州「道の駅」ガイド（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] {{国道10号}} {{デフォルトソート:みちのえきふせんおこしかけ}} [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ|せんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]] [[Category:2000年開業の施設]]

{{道の駅 |名称=豊前おこしかけ |愛称= |画像=[[ファイル:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] |登録年=[[1998年]] |登録月日=[[4月17日]] |開駅年=[[2000年]] |開駅月日=3月 |事務所開設時間=8:00 - 19:00（4 - 10月）
8:00 - 18:00（11 - 3月） |都道府県名=福岡県 |自治体名=豊前市 |所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1 |座標={{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |国土交通省= |連絡会=916 }} [[ファイル:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|人出で賑わう道の駅豊前おこしかけ（2007年10月）]] '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえき ぶぜんおこしかけ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸にある[[国道10号]]の[[道の駅]]である。 国道10号で初の道の駅として[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）3月に開駅した。 名称の「おこしかけ」は[[神功皇后]]が巡幸の途中、当地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因む。照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞した。 2016年4月、[[東九州自動車道]][[椎田南インターチェンジ]] - [[豊前インターチェンジ]]間が開通したことにより前年比で、利用客数は42.5%減、売上も39.9%減と激減した{{Cite news |title= 道の駅・豊前おこしかけ 来店客が激減 東九州道開通で素通り |newspaper=毎日新聞 |date=2016-06-16 |author=降旗英峰 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}}。 == 施設 == * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 * [[公衆電話]] * 公園 * 休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） * 自動販売機 * 情報コーナー：（7:00 - 19:00） * 直売所：（8:00 - 21:00） * 屋台村：（10:00 - 18:00） == 休館日 == * 1月に2日ほど休業あり == アクセス == * [[国道10号]] - 登録路線 ** {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[東九州自動車道]] （[[椎田道路]]）- [[椎田南インターチェンジ|椎田南IC]]椎田南ICは、北九州方面のみの[[ハーフインターチェンジ|ハーフIC]]のため、大分方面からは[[豊前インターチェンジ|豊前IC]]経由。 ** [[福岡県道113号中津豊前線]] == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] * [[道の駅一覧 は行]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|name=豊前おこしかけ}} * [http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html 道の駅 豊前おこしかけ（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] {{国道10号}} {{デフォルトソート:みちのえきふせんおこしかけ}} [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ|せんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]] [[Category:2000年開業の道路施設]] [[Category:国道10号]]

{{道の駅 |名称=豊前おこしかけ |愛称= |画像=[[ファイル:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] |登録年=[[1998年]] |登録月日=[[4月17日]] |開駅年=[[2000年]] |開駅月日=3月 |事務所開設時間=8:00 - 19:00（4 - 10月）
8:00 - 18:00（11 - 3月） |都道府県名=福岡県 |自治体名=豊前市 |所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1 |座標={{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |国土交通省= |連絡会=19696 }} [[ファイル:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|人出で賑わう道の駅豊前おこしかけ（2007年10月）]] '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえき ぶぜんおこしかけ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸にある[[国道10号]]の[[道の駅]]である。 国道10号で初の道の駅として[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）3月に開駅した。 名称の「おこしかけ」は[[神功皇后]]が巡幸の途中、当地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因む。照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞した。 2016年4月、[[東九州自動車道]][[椎田南インターチェンジ]] - [[豊前インターチェンジ]]間が開通したことにより前年比で、利用客数は42.5%減、売上も39.9%減と激減した{{Cite news |title= 道の駅・豊前おこしかけ 来店客が激減 東九州道開通で素通り |newspaper=毎日新聞 |date=2016-06-16 |author=降旗英峰 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}}。 == 施設 == * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 * [[公衆電話]] * 公園 * 休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） * 自動販売機 * 情報コーナー：（7:00 - 19:00） * 直売所：（8:00 - 21:00） * 屋台村：（10:00 - 18:00） == 休館日 == * 1月に2日ほど休業あり == アクセス == * [[国道10号]] - 登録路線 ** {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[東九州自動車道]] （[[椎田道路]]）- [[椎田南インターチェンジ|椎田南IC]]椎田南ICは、北九州方面のみの[[ハーフインターチェンジ|ハーフIC]]のため、大分方面からは[[豊前インターチェンジ|豊前IC]]経由。 ** [[福岡県道113号中津豊前線]] == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] * [[道の駅一覧 は行]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|name=豊前おこしかけ}} * [http://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html 道の駅 豊前おこしかけ（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] {{国道10号}} {{デフォルトソート:みちのえきふせんおこしかけ}} [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ|せんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]] [[Category:2000年開業の道路施設]] [[Category:国道10号]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ** Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ** Large vehicles: 10 spaces ** Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ** Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ** Female: 14 stalls ** Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ** {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. {{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == Related Topics == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]]

It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == Related Topics == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:dardar]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]]

In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == Related Topics == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:New pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]]

''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == Related Topics == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]]

8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == Related Topics == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]]

In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == Related Topics == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]]

8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. {{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake-qq]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]]

{{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]]

8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. 8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake}}[[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake}}

{{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 {{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[en:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake}}

In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[en:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake}} [[Category:Ja import failed]]

revision.text:
{{道の駅 |名称=豊前おこしかけ |愛称= |画像=[[ファイル:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] |登録年=[[1998年]]（[[平成]]10年） |登録月日=[[4月17日]] |開駅年=[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年） |開駅月日=[[3月]] |事務所開設時間=8:00 - 19:00（4 - 10月）
8:00 - 18:00（11 - 3月） |都道府県名=福岡県 |自治体名=豊前市 |所在地=大字四郎丸1041-1 |座標={{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |国土交通省= |連絡会=19696 }} [[ファイル:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|人出で賑わう道の駅豊前おこしかけ（2007年10月）]] '''道の駅豊前おこしかけ'''（みちのえき ぶぜんおこしかけ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]四郎丸にある[[国道10号]]の[[道の駅]]である。 国道10号で初の道の駅として[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）3月に開駅した。 名称の「おこしかけ」は[[神功皇后]]が巡幸の途中、当地で石にこしかけたという伝説に因む。照明学会照明普及会より2000年度の照明普及賞（優秀施設賞）を受賞した。 2016年4月、[[東九州自動車道]][[椎田南インターチェンジ]] - [[豊前インターチェンジ]]間が開通したことにより前年比で、利用客数は42.5%減、売上も39.9%減と激減した{{Cite news |title= 道の駅・豊前おこしかけ 来店客が激減 東九州道開通で素通り |newspaper=毎日新聞 |date=2016-06-16 |author=降旗英峰 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}}。 == 施設 == * [[駐車場]] ** 普通車：72台 ** 大型車：10台 ** 身障者用：4台 * [[便所|トイレ]] （いずれも24時間利用可能）（開駅当時「日本一思いやりのあるトイレ」と大々的に宣伝していた） ** 男：大 4器、小 8器 ** 女：14器 ** 身障者用：2器 * [[公衆電話]] * 公園 * 休憩所（屋外・屋根付き） * 自動販売機 * 情報コーナー：（7:00 - 19:00） * 直売所：（8:00 - 21:00） * 屋台村：（10:00 - 18:00） == 休館日 == * 1月に2日ほど休業あり == アクセス == * [[国道10号]] - 登録路線 ** {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[東九州自動車道]] （[[椎田道路]]）- [[椎田南インターチェンジ|椎田南IC]]椎田南ICは、北九州方面のみの[[ハーフインターチェンジ|ハーフIC]]のため、大分方面からは[[豊前インターチェンジ|豊前IC]]経由。 ** [[福岡県道113号中津豊前線]] == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[道の駅一覧 九州地方]] * [[道の駅一覧 は行]] == 外部リンク == * {{Official website|name=豊前おこしかけ}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html 道の駅 豊前おこしかけ（国土交通省九州地方整備局）] {{国道10号}} {{デフォルトソート:みちのえきふせんおこしかけ}} [[Category:福岡県の道の駅|ふせんおこしかけ]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ|せんおこしかけ]] [[Category:豊前市]] [[Category:2000年開業の道路施設]] [[Category:国道10号]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[en:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake}} [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] {{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations, H]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Facilities along National Route 10]] [[Category:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[en:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[sn: failed]]

''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[en:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:2000年開業の道路施設]] [[Category:国道10号]] [[Category:福岡県の道の駅]]

It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Roadside Stations in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis but with jawiki links]][[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis but with jawiki links]][[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:2000年開業の道路施設]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[National Route 10]]. It opened in [[March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:2000年開業の道路施設]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] {{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

In April 2016, following the opening of the [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:2000年開業の道路施設]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:道の駅 ふ]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:国道10号]] [[Category:福岡県の道の駅]]
