In April 2016, following the opening of the [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Road Facilities Opened in 2000]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations starting with Fu]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:国道10号]] [[Category:福岡県の道の駅]]

It opened in [[:en:March 2000|March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[:en:Parking|Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[Public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[:en:National Route 10|National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Road Facilities Opened in 2000]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations starting with Fu]] [[sn:Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]]

In April 2016, following the opening of the [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[:en:Parking|Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[:en:Restrooms|Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[:en:public telephone|public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[:en:National Route 10|National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Road Facilities Opened in 2000]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations starting with Fu]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11641502}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[:en:Parking|Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[:en:Restrooms|Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[:en:public telephone|public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[:en:National Route 10|National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Road Facilities Opened in 2000]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Roadside Stations starting with Fu]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11641502}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

It opened in [[:en:March 2000|March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[:en:Parking|Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[:en:Restrooms|Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[:en:public telephone|public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[:en:National Route 10|National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. revision.text:
[[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11641502]] Wikidata name: Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Roadside Station | name = Buzen Okoshikake | image = [[File:Michinoeki buzen-okoshikake.jpg|270px|Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake]] | nickname = | registration_year = [[1998]] (Heisei 10) | registration_date = [[April 17]] | opening_year = [[2000]] (Heisei 12) | opening_date = [[March]] | office_hours = 8:00–19:00 (April–October)
8:00–18:00 (November–March) | prefecture = Fukuoka Prefecture | municipality = Buzen City | address = 1041-1, Shiromaru, Ōaza, Buzen-shi | coordinates = {{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | ministry = | association = 19696 }} [[File:Rs-fukuoka-buzen.jpg|thumb|The bustling Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (October 2007)]] '''Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake''' (道の駅 豊前おこしかけ) is a roadside station located in Shiromaru, Buzen City, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], along [[:en:National Route 10|National Route 10]]. It opened in [[:en:March 2000|March 2000]] (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * [[:en:Parking|Parking]] ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[:en:Restrooms|Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * [[:en:public telephone|public telephone]] * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[:en:National Route 10|National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] {{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11641502}} [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Road Facilities Opened in 2000]] [[Category:Roadside Stations starting with Fu]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] {{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11641502}} [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Road Facilities Opened in 2000]] [[Category:Roadside Stations starting with Fu]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It opened in {{ill|March 2000|en|March 2000|WD=Q1616972|lt=March 2000}} (Heisei 12) as the first roadside station on National Route 10. The name "Okoshikake" derives from a local legend in which {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} rested on a stone in the area during her imperial tour. The facility was awarded the 2000 Lighting Encouragement Award (Outstanding Facility Award) by the Japan Illuminating Engineering Institute. In April 2016, following the opening of the [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] between [[Shiida-Minami Interchange]] and [[Buzen Interchange]], the number of visitors decreased by 42.5% compared to the previous year, with sales dropping by 39.9%.{{Cite news |title= Visitor Numbers Drop Sharply at Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake After Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Opening |newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun |date=2016-06-16 |author=Eimei Furihata |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20160616/ddl/k40/020/491000c |accessdate=2017-08-03}} == Facilities == * {{ill|Parking|en|Parking|WD=Q267917|lt=Parking|1=Parking}} ''' Standard vehicles: 72 spaces ''' Large vehicles: 10 spaces ''' Accessible spaces: 4 spaces * [[:en:Restrooms|Restrooms]] (available 24 hours, originally advertised as "Japan's Most Considerate Restroom") ''' Male: 4 large stalls, 8 urinals ''' Female: 14 stalls ''' Accessible: 2 stalls * {{ill|public telephone|en|public telephone|WD=Q16873786|lt=public telephone|1=Public telephone}} * Park * Outdoor rest area (with roof) * Vending machines * Information corner (7:00–19:00) * Direct sales area (8:00–21:00) * Food stalls (10:00–18:00) == Closing Days == * Closed for two days in January. == Access == * [[:en:National Route 10|National Route 10]] (registered route) ''' {{Ja Exp Route Sign|E10}} [[:en:Higashi-Kyushu Expressway|Higashi-Kyushu Expressway]] ([[Shiida Road]]) - [[Shiida-Minami Interchange|Shiida-Minami IC]]Note: Shiida-Minami IC is a [[half interchange]], serving only traffic heading toward Kitakyushu. For traffic from Ōita, use the [[Buzen Interchange|Buzen IC]]. ''' [[Fukuoka Prefectural Route 113 Nakatsu-Buzen Line]] == References == == See Also == * [[List of Roadside Stations in Kyushu]] * [[List of Roadside Stations, H]] == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Buzen Okoshikake}} * [https://www.qsr.mlit.go.jp/n-michi/michi_no_eki/kobetu/buzen/buzen.html Roadside Station Buzen Okoshikake (Kyushu Regional Development Bureau, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism)] {{National Route 10}} [[ja:道の駅豊前おこしかけ]] [[ja:道の駅 豊前おこしかけ]] {{translated page|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|version=97885286|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11641502}} [[Category:2000 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buzen, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Michinoeki in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]] [[Category:Road Facilities Opened in 2000]] [[Category:Roadside Stations starting with Fu]] [[Category:Route 10 (Japan)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
